candidates.notes: Pure word-count chunker with overlap. Identical copy also exists in agent_book_miner.py (line 196-209) — two files, same function, zero differentiation.

candidates.current_implementation: hand-rolled token-bucket rate limiter: Map<string, Bucket> with manual refill-on-consume math
candidates.notes: Token bucket per tenant+agent is standard. rate-limiter-flexible covers this exactly. Swap risk: multi-key composite (tenantId:agentId) would need mapping, but the library supports it.
candidates.evidence: class RateLimiter with Map<string, Bucket> at line 18; manual token refill: `bucket.tokens = Math.min(bucket.max, bucket.tokens + elapsed * bucket.refillRate)` at line 43; full 65-line implementation

candidates.current_implementation: hand-rolled structured JSON logger: LEVEL_ORDER map, log() function, logger object with debug/info/warn/error
candidates.notes: 48-line custom logger reinventing what pino does. No aegis-specific fields that require custom logic. Direct swap.
candidates.evidence: LEVEL_ORDER constant at line 11; custom log() at line 21; logger object at line 43; outputs JSON via console.log/console.error

candidates.current_implementation: hand-rolled TTL in-memory cache: Map<string, CacheEntry> with expiresAt timestamp and manual expiry checks
candidates.notes: Standard TTL cache pattern. lru-cache v10 has native TTL support and bounded size. The domain-key structure (tenant:agent:action:mode) maps cleanly to a string key.
candidates.evidence: class DecisionCache with `private cache: Map<string, CacheEntry>` at line 16; `expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs` at line 20; manual expiry check at line 32

candidates.current_implementation: raw http.server with BaseHTTPRequestHandler; manual do_POST/do_GET dispatch; manual JSON serialization in _send_json
candidates.notes: UASC was explicitly noted as 'raw http.server — NO framework declared' in the task brief. 4 endpoints, HMAC auth middleware. FastAPI swap is straightforward.
candidates.evidence: import `from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler` at line 13; class RequestHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler) at line 204; manual do_POST at line 207; manual do_GET at line 213; manual JSON response at line 260-265

candidates.current_implementation: hand-rolled asyncio tick-loop scheduler: while-not-stop, tick counter, modulo for cadence, hour-of-day comparisons for time-of-day jobs
candidates.notes: The scheduler cadence map (tick modulo + hour-of-day) is simple but the integration with asyncio cancellation and the stop event is load-bearing. APScheduler v4 (async) covers this but the migration would require restructuring the run() coroutine. Flag but defer.
candidates.evidence: class InpactScheduler with asyncio.Event _stop at line 43; while not self._stop.is_set() loop at line 101; tick modulo cadence at lines 73-78; hour-of-day checks at lines 81-88

clean_subsystems: services/canvas-engine (uses zod+ajv+express correctly; only console.log is in server.ts startup, not app code)
services/cortex (uses fastapi+pydantic+nats-py; no hand-rolls in core execution path)
services/optogon (uses fastapi+pydantic+jsonschema; session store manual cache is domain-appropriate wrapper over sqlite)
services/perception (pure library, zero runtime deps, stubs only — no hand-rolls yet)
services/triangulation (numpy-only visual similarity is integration-depth moat, not a solved-category swap)

notes.crucix_bridge_polling: cognitive-sensor/crucix_bridge.py has a while-True/time.sleep polling loop (line 348) but this is a single-purpose bridge script, not a general scheduler — low ROI to swap.
notes.cognitive_sensor_logging: cognitive-sensor uses Python stdlib `logging` throughout (~109 files). This is appropriate stdlib use, not a hand-roll. loguru would be a quality-of-life upgrade but is not a reinvention finding.
